metadata.summary: Synthesize data-backed personas from analytics, interviews, surveys using two-pass agent clustering and validation.

content.title: AI-Assisted Persona Building
content.summary: Synthesize data-backed user personas from multi-source inputs (analytics CSVs, interview transcripts, survey exports) using a two-pass agent pipeline: a Sonnet subagent clusters behavioral patterns and produces draft persona JSON, then a validation agent checks each persona for JTBD consistency and flags fabricated fields. Human researchers approve before personas enter any design system.

why.p: Assumption-driven personas produce design decisions that miss real user behavior. AI behavioral clustering on actual data produces personas grounded in evidence, with quantified pain points and JTBD statements. Two-pass validation catches hallucinations before they propagate.

list.item: You have real user data (analytics events, interview transcripts, survey CSVs) to cluster
You need to synthesize personas from multi-source data faster than manual affinity mapping
A product team wants data-backed personas replacing assumption archetypes
Updating stale personas after a major product pivot or new market entry
Running a JTBD framing exercise alongside persona creation
Zero real user data exists — AI will hallucinate plausible-sounding but false personas
Product is in pre-discovery with no interviews or analytics yet
Regulatory context prohibits feeding user data to third-party LLMs (HIPAA, GDPR special categories)
Only 1–2 archetypes needed from a single homogeneous segment — manual synthesis is faster

section.name: data-driven-process
section.p: 5-step process for building AI-assisted personas from real user data with JTBD integration and modern component structure.

list.item: 1. Aggregate user data: analytics events, interview transcripts, survey exports, support tickets
2. AI behavioral clustering: group users by what they do, then separately by why they do it
3. AI-generated persona drafts in JSON with JTBD statements and pain points
4. Human validation and refinement: check JTBD consistency, flag fabricated fields
5. Ongoing persona updates as new data arrives — version with date and data source reference

rule.statement: Every persona must have exactly one core JTBD statement; multiple JTBDs signal the cluster should be split.
rule.rationale: Multiple JTBDs per persona produce unfocused design decisions. A single JTBD is a testable hypothesis about user motivation.

section.p: Modern persona components: Demographics (age, location, digital behavior patterns), Goals (what they want + JTBD framework statement), Pain points (frustrations + quantified impact from real measurement data only), Behavior (how they act + trigger event analysis), Quotes (representative verbatim quotes + sentiment patterns).

rule.statement: Run two clustering passes: first by behavior (what users do), then by motivation (why they do it). Merge results manually.
rule.rationale: Single-pass clustering produces flat, undifferentiated segments. Two passes surface the behavioral-motivational gap that drives design decisions.

rule.statement: Flag clusters with fewer than 30 data points as thin — do not force-fit personas from insufficient evidence.
rule.rationale: Clustering quality degrades sharply below 30 data points. Thin clusters produce confident-sounding but unreliable personas.

section.name: limitations-and-antipatterns
section.p: AI clusters behavioral patterns but cannot infer motivation without interview quotes or qualitative input. Quantified impact fields ("saves 3 hours/week") require actual measurement data; AI will fabricate numbers if none supplied. Sentiment pattern extraction is unreliable for short text samples (less than 50 responses) — clusters degrade to noise. Trigger analysis requires longitudinal data; single-point surveys produce flat, undifferentiated personas. LLMs over-index on articulate user quotes; silent users who churn silently are systematically under-represented. Persona freshness decays fast; AI-generated personas go stale as fast as their source data.

antipattern.description: Feeding only behavioral data and expecting the agent to infer motivation without interview quotes.
antipattern.reason: AI clusters behavioral patterns but cannot infer motivation from event data alone. Without qualitative input, JTBD statements are fabricated.

antipattern.description: Agent merges two legitimately distinct segments because their surface language is similar.
antipattern.reason: Similar vocabulary in two user groups does not mean similar motivation. Require the agent to explicitly state cluster-separation justification before accepting merged output.

antipattern.description: Passing only new data when updating existing personas, without including the prior persona JSON.
antipattern.reason: Without the prior persona as context, the agent restarts clustering from scratch and produces inconsistent results that cannot be compared to the previous version.

antipattern.description: Using AI personas as the sole input for major product decisions without human interview validation.
antipattern.reason: AI personas are a synthesis tool, not a research substitute. LLMs over-index on articulate user quotes; silent users who churn are systematically under-represented.

antipattern.description: Accepting quantified impact claims (e.g., "saves 3 hours/week") that were not in the input data.
antipattern.reason: AI fabricates specific numbers when quantified fields are requested but no measurement data was supplied. These numbers appear credible and propagate into product decisions.

antipattern.description: Feeding user data to third-party LLMs in HIPAA/GDPR-sensitive contexts without reviewing legal basis.
antipattern.reason: Regulatory context may prohibit sending user data to external LLM providers. Verify lawful basis and consent before any data upload to cloud services.

section.name: agent-pipeline
section.p: Two-pass agent pipeline for persona synthesis. Pass 1: Sonnet subagent ingests structured data (CSV, JSON transcripts, survey exports), perform behavioral segmentation, output draft persona JSON array. Pass 2: Validation subagent checks each persona for JTBD consistency, plausible demographics, flag fabricated fields. Human checkpoint: researcher reviews both outputs, approves before personas enter design system.

rule.statement: Supply real verbatim quotes as grounding material; explicitly forbid the LLM from adding quotes not present in input.
rule.rationale: LLMs confidently fabricate representative quotes that sound authentic. The only way to prevent this is an explicit prohibition in the system prompt.

rule.statement: Preprocess interview transcripts to first-person voice before feeding to the clustering agent.
rule.rationale: JTBD extraction requires first-person voice. Passive or third-person interview notes produce goal lists, not true JTBD statements.

rule.statement: Validate AI-generated quantified claims (time saved, frequency) against actual analytics before publishing.
rule.rationale: LLMs fabricate plausible-sounding numbers when quantified impact fields are requested but no measurement data was supplied.

rule.statement: Store persona JSON in a structured registry with date and data source reference so staleness is visible.
rule.rationale: Persona freshness decays as fast as its source data. Versioning with a timestamp makes outdated personas detectable before they influence decisions.

section.p: Prompt pattern for clustering: You are a UX research analyst. Given the following user interview transcripts and analytics segments, identify 3–5 distinct behavioral clusters. For each cluster output: Cluster label, Core JTBD statement (When [situation], I want to [motivation], So I can [outcome]), Top 3 pain points with severity (High/Med/Low), Trigger events that activate product usage, Representative verbatim quote. Output as JSON array. Do not invent data not present in the input.

Prompt pattern for validation: You are reviewing persona drafts for internal consistency. For each persona check: (1) Is the JTBD statement consistent with the pain points listed? (2) Are the behavior patterns plausible given the demographics? (3) Flag any fields that appear fabricated rather than data-derived. Output issues as a numbered list per persona.

CLI tools: jq transforms analytics JSON exports before feeding to LLM via apt install jq from jq.dev. csvkit converts survey CSV to normalized JSON for LLM input via pip install csvkit from csvkit.rtfd.io. python-docx and pypdf extract interview transcripts from Word/PDF files via pip install python-docx pypdf.

Services: Dovetail (SaaS qualitative research) has partial agent support with REST API for notes/tags; export tags + themes as input to LLM clustering. Maze (SaaS usability/surveys) is agent-friendly with CSV/JSON export for survey data source for segmentation. Mixpanel and Amplitude (SaaS analytics) are agent-friendly with API and CSV export for behavioral event data for cluster features. Notion (SaaS docs) has API support to store validated persona cards where agents can read/update. Airtable (SaaS structured DB) provides REST API for structured persona registry and agent-updatable records. Atlas (Anthropic SaaS LLM ops) tracks persona generation prompts for auditability.

example.title: Normalize interviews for LLM clustering
example.note: Preprocess interview JSON to standard records format for LLM input.

section.name: best-practices
section.p: Always supply real verbatim quotes as grounding material; forbid LLM from adding quotes not present in input. Run two clustering passes: first by behavior (what users do), then by motivation (why they do it) — merge results manually. Limit each persona to 1 core JTBD statement; multiple JTBDs signal the cluster should be split. Version personas with a date and data source reference so staleness is visible. Validate AI-generated quantified claims (time saved, frequency) against actual analytics before publishing. Store persona JSON in a structured registry (Airtable, Notion DB) so agents can query and update incrementally. Never use AI personas as the sole input for major product decisions — require at least 1 round of human interview validation.

LLMs confidently hallucinate demographic details when given only behavioral data — require explicit "only use provided data" constraint in prompt. Clustering quality degrades sharply with fewer than 30 data points per segment; instruct agent to flag thin clusters rather than force-fit. Agent may merge two legitimately distinct segments if their surface language is similar — require explicit cluster-separation justification. JTBD extraction requires first-person voice in input; passive or third-person interview notes produce weak statements — preprocess before feeding. Iterative persona updates (new data → update existing personas) require the agent to receive the prior persona JSON explicitly; without it, the agent restarts clustering from scratch and produces inconsistent results. Human-in-loop checkpoint required before personas enter design system or stakeholder decks.

template.purpose: Preprocessing script: interview JSON → normalized records for LLM input
Sonnet prompt for behavioral clustering and persona draft generation
Validation agent prompt to check JTBD consistency and flag fabricated fields
